title: cyperorchis × fowlerae
intro: Cyperorchis × fowlerae, also known as the Fowler's orchid or Fowler's cyper orchid, is a rare hybrid orchid species resulting from the cross between Cypripedium parviflorum and Calopogon barbatus. The medicinal properties of Cyperorchis × fowlerae include its potential anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects, which can be attributed to the presence of flavonoids and alkaloids in the plant. The health benefits associated with this plant include its possible use in treating respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and asthma, due to its expectorant properties. Cyperorchis × fowlerae can be prepared through herbal preparations such as infusions, tinctures, or decoctions, but further research is needed to confirm its efficacy and safety for human consumption.
